title: Plagiobothrys calandrinioides
taxonomy: Plagiobothrys calandrinioides belongs to the family Boraginaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Plagiobothrys. The genus Plagiobothrys is part of the order Borales and the class Magnoliopsida. The kingdom to which it belongs is Plantae. This classification places Plagiobothrys calandrinioides as a member of the plant kingdom.

intro: Plagiobothrys calandrinioides, also known as Calandrin's poppy gromwell, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Boraginaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Plagiobothrys calandrinioides include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat coughs, colds, and skin conditions.
